column7: Контролер не відповідає. Стан контролера {0}. Перезапустити?
Probe over the loaded job's XY extent, in the current work coordinates.
Probe the whole in-bounds machine travel envelope (referenced to the homed corner, like Surface spoilboard).
How far DOWN from the parked (start) Z each probe searches before it faults. Park Z at a small safe clearance above the surface, then this must be at least that clearance plus the surface variation.

Feed for every probe. The surface is already roughly known, so all points probe gently at this single approach feed - no fast search pass - which avoids overshoot/false triggers on a fragile probe. Defaults to the probe's latch (slow) feed.
Pause before each probe so a fragile/3D probe fully releases and stops ringing after the previous touch and the move. Raise this if you get 'probe already triggered' fails partway through. 0 = none.
Copy the measured stock size (X Y [Z] in mm) to the clipboard for Fusion's ioSenderBatchPost 'measured stock' field.
Probe all four corners to measure the true stock size and skew. When off, the width/height above are used as-is and only the front-left origin (and optional TLO reference) is set.
Store the measured stock skew as the work coordinate system's rotation (G10 L2 R), so any program run against this stock is cut aligned to it. Requires firmware with ROTATION_ENABLE.
Reference the tool length at the toolsetter puck (M6 T8, probe already in the spindle) after the origin is set - the core of a one-button job start. Requires ATC and a toolsetter at G59.3.
After a measure run: re-apply the measured origin + skew rotation, then touch each corner in the rotated work frame. If the skew is right (and the stock square) the probe lands right at every corner.
Open the per-user folder where ioSender keeps its settings (App.config), key mappings, grbl backups and restore points.
Copy the full status message to the clipboard
